perturbation.original_text: This Agreement is for a term (the "Term") of 12 months from the Effective Date on June 1s t 2020 and expiring May 31st 2021. (a) In the case that the company would not like to extend the terms of agreement for an additional month. The company must notify the consultant within 5 days of the conclusion of the 12 month term. Without notification the contract will automatically extend for an additional month of service.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement is for a term (the "Term") of 12 months from the Effective Date on June 1s t 2020 and expiring May 31st 2021. (a) In the case that the company would not like to extend the terms of agreement for an additional month. The company must notify the consultant within 1 day of the conclusion of the 12 month term. Without notification the contract will automatically extend for an additional month of service.
perturbation.explanation: Reducing the notification period for non-renewal to 1 day may be unreasonably short, particularly in New York, where employment and contractual relationships are subject to implied duties of good faith and fair dealing, and may be deemed insufficient notice depending on the circumstances and industry standards.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Implied Covenant of Good Faith and Fair Dealing under New York Law
perturbation.law_explanation: New York law implies a covenant of good faith and fair dealing in every contract. Reducing the notification period to 1 day may be considered a breach of this covenant if it is deemed unreasonably short and deprives the consultant of the benefit of the agreement.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: NYS Open Legislation | NYSenate.gov Sorry, you need to enable JavaScript to visit this website. Skip to main content Legislation Search OpenLegislation Statutes Search Term Search icon Search The Laws of New York Consolidated Laws of New York CHAPTER 38 Uniform Commercial Code ARTICLE 1 No title PART 3 Territorial Applicability and General Rules previous SECTION 1-303 Course of Performance, Course of Dealing, and Usage of Trade up PART 3 Territorial Applicability and General Rules next SECTION 1-305 Remedies to be Liberally Administered This entry was published on 2014-12-26 The selection dates indicate all change milestones for the entire volume, not just the location being viewed. Specifying a milestone date will retrieve the most recent version of the location before that date. See most recent version before or on: 2014-09-22 2014-12-26 2015-01-02 2015-02-20 2015-02-27 2016-05-20 2016-08-26 2016-09-16 2018-08-31 2018-10-05 2021-12-03 2022-01-28 2022-06-03 Share Facebook Email SECTION 1-304 Obligation of Good Faith Uniform Commercial Code (UCC) CHAPTER 38, ARTICLE 1, PART 3 Section 1--304. Obligation of Good Faith. Every contract or duty within this act imposes an obligation of good faith in its performance and enforcement.
perturbation.justification: The modified text reduces the notification period for non-renewal to 1 day. The Law Explanation states that this may be a breach of the implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing if deemed unreasonably short and deprives the consultant of the benefit of the agreement. Scraped Law Snippet 1 states: "Every contract or duty within this act imposes an obligation of good faith in its performance and enforcement." This suggests the changed text, by reducing the notification period to one day, creates a potential violation of the implied covenant of good faith, creating a legal contradiction under 'Inconsistencies Legal Contradiction', which states that such contradictions occur when time-sensitive obligations violate state law.

perturbation.original_text: A total of 100,000 RSA's ("Restricted Stock Awards") that vest immediately, subject to prior approval of the Board of Directors.
perturbation.changed_text: A total of 100,000 RSA's ("Restricted Stock Awards") that vest immediately, with no prior approval of the Board of Directors.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the 'subject to prior approval of the Board of Directors' clause might violate corporate governance regulations or bylaws that require board approval for equity grants, potentially leading to legal challenges regarding the validity of the stock awards.
perturbation.law_explanation: Delaware law (where many corporations are incorporated) generally requires board approval or authorization for the issuance of stock, including restricted stock awards. Removing this requirement contradicts standard corporate governance procedures and potentially violates Delaware law if the corporation is incorporated there, especially Section 153, which deals with consideration for stock.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: Delaware Code Online Title 8 > Chapter 1 Authenticated PDF Â§ 151 Â§ 152 Â§ 153 Â§ 154 Â§ 155 Â§ 156 Â§ 157 Â§ 158 Â§ 159 Â§ 160 Â§ 161 Â§ 162 Â§ 163 Â§ 164 Â§ 165 Â§ 166 Â§ 167 Â§ 168 Â§ 169 Â§ 170 Â§ 171 Â§ 172 Â§ 173 Â§ 174 TITLE 8 Corporations CHAPTER 1. General Corporation Law Subchapter V. Stock and Dividends Â§ 151. Classes and series of stock; redemption; rights. (a) Every corporation may issue 1 or more classes of stock or 1 or more series of stock within any class thereof, any or all of which classes may be of stock with par value or stock without par value and which classes or series may have such voting powers, full or limited, or no voting powers, and such designations, preferences and relative, participating, optional or other special rights, and qualifications, limitations or restrictions thereof, as shall be stated and expressed in the certificate of incorporation or of any amendment thereto, or in the resolution or resolutions providing for the issue of such stock adopted by the board of directors pursuant to authority expressly vested in it by the provisions of its certificate of incorporation. Any of the voting powers, designations, preferences, rights and qualifications, limitations or restrictions of any such class or series of stock may be made dependent upon facts ascertainable outside the certificate of incorporation or of any amendment thereto, or outside the resolution or resolutions providing for the issue of such stock adopted by the board of directors pursuant to authority expressly vested in it by its certificate of incorporation, provided that the manner in which such facts shall operate upon the voting powers, designations, preferences, rights and qualifications, limitations or restrictions of such class or series of stock is clearly and expressly set forth in the certificate of incorporation or in the resolution or resolutions providing for the issue of such stock adopted by the board of directors. perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: 8 Delaware Code § 153 (2023) - Consideration for stock. :: 2023 Delaware Code :: U.S. Codes and Statutes :: U.S. Law :: Justia There Is a Newer Version of this Section 2024 Delaware Code 2023 Delaware Code (here) 2022 Delaware Code View All Versions View Our Newest Version Here 2023 Delaware Code Title 8 - Corporations Chapter 1. GENERAL CORPORATION LAW Subchapter V. Stock and Dividends § 153. Consideration for stock. Universal Citation: 8 DE Code § 153 (2023) Learn more This media-neutral citation is based on the American Association of Law Libraries Universal Citation Guide and is not necessarily the official citation. Previous Next § 153. Consideration for stock. (a) Shares of stock with par value may be issued for such consideration, having a value not less than the par value of the shares so issued, as determined from time to time in accordance with § 152 of this title, or by the stockholders if the certificate of incorporation so provides. (b) Shares of stock without par value may be issued for such consideration as is determined from time to time in accordance with § 152 of this title, or by the stockholders if the certificate of incorporation so provides. (c) Treasury shares may be disposed of by the corporation in the same manner that shares of stock are issued pursuant to § 152(a) through (d) of this title, or may be disposed of for such consideration as determined by the stockholders if the certificate of incorporation so provides. The consideration received for treasury shares may have a value greater or less than, or equal to, the par value (if any) of such shares and may consist of cash, any tangible or intangible property or any benefit to the corporation, or any combination thereof. (d) If the certificate of incorporation reserves to the stockholders the right to determine the consideration for the issue of any shares, the stockholders shall, unless the certificate requires a greater vote, do so by a vote of a majority of the outstanding stock entitled to vote thereon. 8
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the requirement for Board of Directors approval for RSA's, which contradicts Delaware law (specifically 8 DE Code § 153) that mandates consideration for stock to be determined by the board or stockholders, as stated in the Law Explanation. While § 153 itself doesn't explicitly state Board approval is always needed, it implies a process involving the board or stockholders in determining the consideration for stock issuance. Removing the Board approval clause directly undermines the standard corporate governance procedures and potentially violates Delaware law if the corporation is incorporated there.

perturbation.original_text: The Company shall provide disclosures in each of its Forms 10-K and 10-Q as to the existence of this Agreement and any Statement of Work, the amount paid or to be paid in connection with each Statement of Work and the types of services to be provided under each Statement of Work.
perturbation.changed_text: The Company may, at its discretion, provide disclosures in its Forms 10-K and 10-Q as to the existence of this Agreement and any Statement of Work, the amount paid or to be paid in connection with each Statement of Work and the types of services to be provided under each Statement of Work.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'shall' to 'may' makes the disclosure discretionary, which could violate SEC regulations requiring disclosure of material contracts and related party transactions. This creates non-compliance as it might not transparently report important financial relationships, misleading investors and the public.
perturbation.law_explanation: Item 601 of Regulation S-K mandates the filing of material contracts. Making the disclosure of this consulting agreement discretionary contradicts this requirement, as the agreement's terms and payments could be considered material information that must be disclosed to investors.

perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: 17 CFR § 229.601 - (Item 601) Exhibits. | Electronic Code of Federal Regulations (e-CFR) | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you 17 CFR § 229.601 - (Item 601) Exhibits. CFR Table of Popular Names § 229.601 (Item 601) Exhibits. Link to an amendment published at 89 FR 21914 , Mar. 28, 2024. This amendment was delayed indefinitely at 89 FR 25804 , Apr. 12, 2024. (a) Exhibits and index required. (1) Subject to Rule 411(c) ( § 230.411(c) of this chapter) under the Securities Act and Rule 12b-23(c) (§ 240.12b-23(c) of this chapter) under the Exchange Act regarding incorporation of exhibits by reference, the exhibits required in the exhibit table must be filed as indicated, as part of the registration statement or report. (2) Each registration statement or report shall contain an exhibit index, which must appear before the required signatures in the registration statement or report. For convenient reference, each exhibit shall be listed in the exhibit index according to the number assigned to it in the exhibit table. If an exhibit is incorporated by reference, this must be noted in the exhibit index. Each exhibit identified in the exhibit index (other than an exhibit filed in eXtensible Business Reporting Language or an exhibit that is filed with Form ABS-EE) must include an active link to an exhibit that is filed with the registration statement or report or, if the exhibit is incorporated by reference, an active hyperlink to the exhibit separately filed on EDGAR. If a registration statement or report is amended, each amendment must include hyperlinks to the exhibits required with the amendment. For a description of each of the exhibits included in the exhibit table, see paragraph (b) of this section. (3) This Item applies only to the forms specified in the exhibit table. With regard to forms not listed in that table, reference shall be made to the appropriate form for the specific exhibit filing requirements applicable thereto. (4) If
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'shall' with 'may,' making the disclosure of the agreement and related payments discretionary. Item 601 of Regulation S-K requires the filing of material contracts. By making the disclosure optional, the modified text contradicts the requirement to disclose potentially material information, thus creating a legal contradiction. Scraped Law Snippet 2 states, '(a) Exhibits and index required,' indicating a mandatory requirement to file exhibits.
